data.title: ICG Enterprise Trust Continues Share Buyback, Adds to Treasury Holdings
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions

ICG Enterprise Trust PLC GBP ( (GB:ICGT) ) has issued an update.

ICG Enterprise Trust plc has repurchased 3,263 of its own shares on 14 April 2026 at an average price of 1,359 pence per share, with the shares to be held in treasury. Following this transaction, the company holds 2,092,369 shares in treasury and has 61,461,823 shares in issue excluding treasury, as it continues to execute its shareholder-approved buyback programme within limits linked to net asset value.

The buyback is being conducted under authority granted at the June 2025 AGM, allowing repurchases of up to 14.99% of ordinary shares until renewed or revoked. By using Deutsche Numis as broker and committing not to pay above net asset value, the company signals an intention to manage its capital structure efficiently, potentially enhancing net asset value per share and supporting the share price for existing investors.

The most recent analyst rating on (GB:ICGT) stock is a Buy with a £17.90 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on ICG Enterprise Trust PLC GBP stock, see the GB:ICGT Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on ICGT Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, ICGT is a Neutral.

The score is driven mainly by solid financial flexibility (low leverage) but tempered by materially inconsistent cash generation, including negative operating/free cash flow in 2025. Technical indicators point to weak near-term momentum, while valuation looks reasonable and corporate actions (buybacks below NAV plus liquidity-boosting realisations) provide additional support.

To see Spark’s full report on ICGT stock, click here.

More about ICG Enterprise Trust PLC GBP

ICG Enterprise Trust plc is a London-listed investment trust focused on private equity and related investments. The company provides shareholders with exposure to a diversified portfolio of private companies, aiming to deliver long-term capital growth through active portfolio management and disciplined capital allocation.

Average Trading Volume: 82,043

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: £823.8M

data.title: Silver Bullet Turns EBITDA Positive as Q1 Revenue Jumps 22%
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An announcement from Silver Bullet Data Services Group plc ( (GB:SBDS) ) is now available.

Silver Bullet Data Services Group reported a strong start to 2026, with first-quarter revenue up 22% year on year and performance running 9% ahead of budget, driven by higher-quality revenue, improved margins and increased renewal rates. New wins, including a major European airline and a global marketing and technology partner in Asia-Pacific, have reinforced its international reach and deepened key client relationships.

Following cost restructuring at the end of 2025 and greater use of artificial intelligence to boost efficiency, the group delivered positive EBITDA in Q1 2026 for the first time, an improvement of £700,000 on the prior year. Management expects profitability to be sustained and anticipates becoming cash flow positive from the end of the second quarter, bolstering confidence in the company’s strategy and outlook for 2026.

Spark’s Take on SBDS Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, SBDS is a Neutral.

The score is primarily held down by weak financial performance—ongoing losses, high leverage, and negative operating cash flow—despite revenue growth and strong gross margins. Technicals are mixed but lean weak given the price sits below key longer-term moving averages, and valuation is constrained by loss-making earnings and no dividend support.

To see Spark’s full report on SBDS stock, click here.

More about Silver Bullet Data Services Group plc

Silver Bullet Data Services Group plc is a London-headquartered provider of AI-driven digital transformation services and products, focused on helping advertisers navigate a privacy-first advertising landscape. Its proprietary 4D AI advertising solution complements a services business that supports blue-chip clients across sectors, with over 85 data specialists operating in the UK, Italy, Australia, the U.S. and Latin America.

The company has built an established and growing solutions business with significant experience in data engineering, SaaS product development and marketing. Its board is composed largely of executives who previously held senior roles at global software companies, underpinning Silverbullet’s strategy to expand its international footprint and scale its AI-led offerings worldwide.

Average Trading Volume: 131,973

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: £3.63M

The latest update is out from International Public Partnerships ( (GB:INPP) ).

International Public Partnerships Limited has carried out a share buyback on the London Stock Exchange, purchasing 100,000 ordinary shares at 128.0 pence each through Deutsche Numis Securities. The company intends to hold these shares in treasury, reflecting an ongoing capital management strategy that can support earnings per share and signal confidence in the business.

Following this transaction, INPP now holds 115,009,849 ordinary shares in treasury and has 1,796,233,283 ordinary shares in issue, excluding treasury stock. The updated capital structure may influence trading liquidity and per-share metrics, with potential implications for existing shareholders and the market’s perception of the company’s valuation and capital allocation discipline.

The most recent analyst rating on (GB:INPP) stock is a Buy with a £1.45 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on International Public Partnerships stock, see the GB:INPP Stock Forecast page.

The score is primarily supported by strong financial fundamentals—especially a very conservative balance sheet and improving cash generation—tempered by notable earnings volatility. Technicals are neutral-to-slightly weak in the near term, while valuation support comes mainly from the high dividend yield (P/E not usable from the provided data).

To see Spark’s full report on INPP stock, click here.

More about International Public Partnerships

International Public Partnerships Limited (INPP) is a listed infrastructure investment company focused on global public infrastructure projects that address societal and environmental needs. Its diversified portfolio of more than 130 assets spans utilities and transmission, transport, education, health, justice and digital infrastructure across the UK, Europe, Australia, New Zealand and North America, aiming to deliver long-term yield and capital growth for shareholders.

Amber Fund Management Limited acts as INPP’s investment adviser, with a team of around 180 professionals responsible for originating and managing the company’s infrastructure investments. This advisory structure supports INPP’s role as a responsible, long-term investor in essential public infrastructure markets worldwide.

Rank Group plc ( (GB:RNK) ) has shared an announcement.

Rank Group reported a 5% year-on-year rise in like-for-like net gaming revenue to £205.4m for its third quarter ended 31 March 2026, with year-to-date revenue up 6% and growth across all divisions, including Grosvenor casinos, Mecca bingo, Enracha venues and digital operations. Strong profit conversion from this revenue performance has led the company to raise guidance, now expecting full-year underlying like-for-like operating profit of at least £68m, supported by cost-saving measures to offset higher UK Remote Gaming Duty, tax changes that benefit its bingo venues and ongoing improvements in its international digital business, while management reiterates a medium-term goal of generating £100m in operating profit.

To see Spark’s full report on RNK stock, click here.

data.title: Digital 9 Infrastructure Slashes NAV as Arqiva Written Down, Sets First Capital Return
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Digital 9 Infrastructure Plc ( (GB:DGI9) ) has issued an announcement.

Digital 9 Infrastructure reported a sharp fall in net asset value to 9.3 pence per share for 2025, driven by asset disposals and a major write-down of its stake in broadcast and communications group Arqiva to a nil equity value. The group completed the sales of EMIC-1, SeaEdge UK1 and subsea cable operator Aqua Comms for £76.7 million, enabling full repayment and cancellation of its revolving credit facility and leaving the company in a net cash position that materially reduces financial risk.

Following the year end, Digital 9 also secured a £10 million cash settlement on the Verne Global earn-out, supporting the first compulsory share redemption equivalent to about 3.5 pence per share expected by late April 2026. With just Arqiva and data-centre platform Elio Networks remaining in the portfolio, management is focused on maximising value from these assets, with Elio trading ahead of plan, while the Arqiva revaluation reflects more conservative long-term assumptions and market transaction evidence rather than current trading weakness, shaping the scale and timing of future capital returns to investors.

The most recent analyst rating on (GB:DGI9) stock is a Sell with a £0.06 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Digital 9 Infrastructure Plc stock, see the GB:DGI9 Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on DGI9 Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, DGI9 is a Neutral.

The score is driven primarily by weak financial performance, including large recent losses, negative revenue, shrinking equity, and unstable cash flow. Technical signals also lean bearish with price below key moving averages and a negative MACD. Valuation is neutral due to missing P/E and dividend yield data.

To see Spark’s full report on DGI9 stock, click here.

More about Digital 9 Infrastructure Plc

Digital 9 Infrastructure plc is a London-listed investment trust in the digital infrastructure sector and a constituent of the FTSE All-Share index. The company now operates under a managed wind-down mandate, seeking to realise its remaining portfolio investments, primarily in Arqiva and Elio Networks, and return capital to shareholders in an orderly manner.

Average Trading Volume: 2,221,935

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Learn more about DGI9 stock on TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

data.title: Frontier IP portfolio firm 2D Photonics wins €211m state aid to scale graphene photonics
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Frontier IP ( (GB:FIPP) ) has provided an update.

Frontier IP Group, which focuses on commercialising academic and research-based intellectual property through equity stakes in spin-outs, holds a 9.1% interest in 2D Photonics Group, a deep-tech company spun out from the University of Cambridge and Italy’s CNIT. The group’s model centres on early engagement with industry partners to accelerate development and market adoption of advanced technologies.

Portfolio company 2D Photonics has secured approval for €211 million in Italian state aid, one of the largest public investments in an Italian deep-tech start-up, to industrialise its graphene-based photonics technology. The funding will support a pilot manufacturing facility near Milan, job creation, and the scaling of graphene optical interconnects aimed at solving data-movement bottlenecks in AI and high-performance computing, potentially strengthening both Europe’s semiconductor ecosystem and Frontier IP’s portfolio value.

Spark’s Take on FIPP Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, FIPP is a Neutral.

The score is held back primarily by weak financial performance (recurring losses and persistent cash burn, with added leverage risk in 2025). Technicals are a clear positive with strong trend signals, but overbought RSI adds short-term risk. Valuation is mixed because a negative P/E reflects losses and limits valuation confidence.

To see Spark’s full report on FIPP stock, click here.

More about Frontier IP

Frontier IP Group plc is a UK-based specialist in commercialising intellectual property, helping transform academic and research innovations into viable commercial ventures. The group builds a portfolio of equity stakes and licence income in spin-out companies by providing commercialisation services and early-stage support, including fundraising and industry collaboration to ensure technologies meet real-world market needs.

data.title: Lumi Gruppen lifts profitability and expands digital education footprint in 2025
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Lumi Gruppen AS ( (DE:8KS) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Lumi Gruppen AS, a Norwegian provider of flexible, technology-enabled education, reported strong results for 2025, underpinned by growth at Oslo Nye Høyskole and improved profitability at Sonans. The company continues to build out a multi-channel higher education and exam preparation platform, supported by a new Oslo campus and a series of targeted digital acquisitions.

Total income rose 12.5% to NOK 507.7 million, while adjusted EBIT jumped 49% to NOK 89.5 million, lifting the margin to 17.6%. Profit after tax doubled to NOK 28.7 million as Oslo Nye Høyskole delivered 16.1% revenue growth and Sonans returned to growth with higher margins following restructuring.

Strategic acquisitions such as Realfagshjelpen and the planned purchase of Edrupt’s EnkelEksamen platform broaden Lumi’s digital capabilities and course offering. Despite higher operating expenses from personnel, technology investments and credit loss provisions, operating leverage supported margin expansion and underpinned the group’s sound financial position.

Operating cash flow declined to NOK 61.9 million, mainly due to working capital effects and the lack of portfolio sales compared with the prior year. Net debt ended at NOK 181 million with leverage at 2.5x, while available credit and capex facilities of NOK 170 million leave the group room to invest in programmes, platforms, people and further acquisitions.

Management sees structural demand for lifelong learning and flexible education as an ongoing tailwind for the business. The company is maintaining financial ambitions for double-digit revenue growth and EBIT margins above 20%, underscoring confidence in its strategic direction and market positioning.

More about Lumi Gruppen AS

Lumi Gruppen AS is a leading Norwegian education provider focused on flexible, technology-enabled learning through its Oslo Nye Høyskole private university college and Sonans exam preparation division. The group targets fast-growing areas of study demand, with around 80% of ONH students online and a new central Oslo campus, while Sonans leads the market in private high school exam preparation for students seeking improved results or completed diplomas.

Average Trading Volume: 4,020

Current Market Cap: NOK1.17B

Find detailed analytics on 8KS stock on TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

data.title: Australian Retirement Trust Ceases to Be Substantial Holder in Tabcorp
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An update from Tabcorp Holdings Limited ( (AU:TAH) ) is now available.

Australian Retirement Trust Pty Ltd, as trustee for the Australian Retirement Trust, has notified Tabcorp Holdings Limited that it has ceased to be a substantial shareholder in the company as of 13 April 2026. The change, lodged under Australia’s Corporations Act disclosure rules, indicates a reduction in the fund’s voting interest in Tabcorp and may slightly alter the company’s institutional shareholding profile, although no new associations or control arrangements were disclosed.

The notice, signed by company secretary Kristy Huxtable on 15 April 2026, confirms that there are no new or changed associations related to this holding and that the change in interest is detailed in an accompanying annexure. While the operational impact on Tabcorp is not specified, the move reflects normal portfolio rebalancing activity by a major superannuation investor and is relevant for shareholders monitoring shifts in the company’s ownership base.

The most recent analyst rating on (AU:TAH) stock is a Buy with a A$1.20 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Tabcorp Holdings Limited stock, see the AU:TAH Stock Forecast page.

More about Tabcorp Holdings Limited

Tabcorp Holdings Limited is an Australian gambling and entertainment company focused on wagering, lotteries and gaming services. It operates betting and related products primarily in the Australian market, serving both retail and digital customers under a regulated framework.

Average Trading Volume: 7,763,672

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Buy

Current Market Cap: A$2.25B
